sections.paragraph:
ἐκδύω fut. ἐκδύσω LXX; 1 aor. ἐξέδυσα; fut. mid. ἐκδύσομαι LXX; aor. mid. ἐξεδυσάμην (δύω, ‘get into’; Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Test12Patr, JosAs, Philo)

① to remove clothing from the body, strip, take off. Act., w. acc. of pers. (SIG 1169, 47 [IV b.c.]; PMagd 6, 13=PEnteux 75, 13 [221 b.c.]; 1 Ch 10:9; Hos 2:5) Mt 27:28; Lk 10:30. W. acc. of pers. and thing (Gen 37:23; TestJud 3:5; TestZeb 4:10) Mt 27:31; Mk 15:20.—Mid., strip, undress (oneself) abs., lit. (as X., Hell. 3, 4, 19 et al.; Is 32:11) POxy 655, 22 (ASyn. 67, 35=GTh 37). Fig., of the body as a garment (Artem. 5, 40 ἐκ τῶν σαρκῶν ἐκδύνειν) οὐ θέλομεν ἐκδύσασθαι we do not want to strip ourselves 2 Cor 5:4; cp. vs. 3.—Lit. on γυμνός 1.

② to remove by force, plunder, fig. ext. of 1: B 10:4.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

PEnteux PEnteux = Papyrus Enteuxeis—List 4

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

abs. abs. = absolute

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

GTh GTh = Gospel of Thomas in Coptic or in Oxy. documents—Lists 1

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ext. ext. = extension

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 301
